evals.name: new-feature-list-detail
evals.prompt: I'm using go_router_modular in my Flutter app. Create a new 'products' feature module with two screens: a products list at the root and a product detail screen that takes a product id in the path. Wire up the routes and the module. Show me the feature files.
evals.expected_output: A products_module.dart and a products_route.dart. products_route.dart has ProductsRouteRelative (path/name constants: relative path, *Module, *Named, param$id key, productDetail$id path) and ProductsRoute (of(context), kebab-case names, go/push + a push-detail method passing pathParameters, and a static getProductIdParam(state) reader). The module's ChildRoutes each set path from a relative constant and name from a *Named constant. binds is synchronous.

evals.name: navigate-between-pages
evals.prompt: In my go_router_modular app I have a HomePage and I want to add a button that navigates to the user profile screen, which lives at /profile and shows a userId from the path. Write the navigation code.
evals.expected_output: Navigation goes through a *Route class using context.goNamed/pushNamed (e.g. ProfileRoute.of(context).pushProfile(userId: ...)), NOT context.go('/profile') with a raw path string. Path parameters are passed through the named method.

evals.name: module-with-dependencies
evals.prompt: Using go_router_modular, set up a 'checkout' module that depends on a CartRepository and a CheckoutCubit, and imports a shared PaymentModule. Show me the module definition.
evals.expected_output: A Module whose binds(Injector i) registers CartRepository and CheckoutCubit synchronously (void binds, no async/await/Future), and imports() returns [PaymentModule()] synchronously. No async module unless an awaited resource is clearly required.
